title: Timothée Chalamet Says Past Golden Globe Losses Make His Win for Marty Supreme That Much Sweeter
text: Chalamet, 30, won Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy at the 83rd Golden Globes ceremony on January 11, 2026, for his role as 1950s ping-pong hustler Marty Mauser in Josh Safdie's film. This marks his first Golden Globe after four previous nominations. Acceptance Speech Highlights: Chalamet credited his father's influence, stating: My dad instilled in me a spirit of gratitude growing up: Always be grateful for what you have. It's allowed me to leave this ceremony in the past empty-handed, my head held high, grateful just to be here. But I'd be lying if I didn't say those moments didn't make this moment that much sweeter. He also thanked director Safdie and humorously acknowledged Kevin O'Leary (the businessman/Shark Tank personality) in the cast, joking about being shocked to thank him. Chalamet extended gratitude to his parents and his partner Kylie Jenner, who was present at the ceremony. He defeated Leonardo DiCaprio, George Clooney, Jesse Plemons, Ethan Hawke, and Lee Byung-Hun for the award.
